LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JERRIE SPENCER MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE COURT GRANTS THE 1ST AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR DISCRIMINATION CLAIMS IN FAVOR OF THE CITY. THE COURT FINDS THAT MR. ZHANG DID NOT MEET HIS PRIMA FACIE BURDEN TO SHOW THAT HE WAS PERFORMING COMPETENTLY. THE COURT DENIES THE 2ND AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION AND FINDS THERE IS TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO WHETHER THERE WAS SEVERE AND PERVASIVE HARASSMENT. THE COURT GRANTS THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION. MR. ZHANG HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER THE CITY'S NONDISCRIMINATORY REASON WAS PRETEXUAL BASED ON HIS DISABILITY. THE COURT DENIES THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO PREVENT DISCRIMINATION HARASSMENT AND FINDS THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE. THE COURT DENIES THE 7TH AND 8TH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO PROVIDE REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION AND FAILURE TO PROVIDE REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION AND ENGAGE IN AN INTERACTIVE PROCESS. THE COURT FINDS THEY ARE TRIABLE ISSUES AS TO WHETHER THE CITY FAIRLY ADDRESSED THE MEDICAL ISSUES THAT WERE PRESENTED BY MR. ZHANG. THE COURT GRANTS THE 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION, IN FAVOR OF THE CITY. THE PARTIES SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, Jerrie Spencer Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-10-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael H. Kim, of Michael H. Kim, APC, Tel. 650.697.8899, for plaintiffs;
Jennifer Donnellan, of Office of the City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3800, for defendants;
Court Approved Interpreter: Rebecca Chau, Tel. 707.315.1403, (services not required for this hearing).

Ruling: A R G U E D. Defendant City And County Of San Francisco, Jerrie Spencer Motion For Summary Judgment, or in the Alternative Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The Court GRANTS the 1st and 3rd causes of action for discrimination claims in favor of the City. The Court finds that Mr. Zhang did not meet his prima facie burden to show that he was performing competently. The Court DENIES the 2nd and 4th causes of action and finds there is triable issue as to whether there was severe and pervasive harassment. The Court GRANTS the 5th cause of action for disability discrimination. Mr. Zhang has raised a triable issue whether the City's nondiscriminatory reason was pretexual based on his disability. The Court DENIES the 6th cause of action for failure to prevent discrimination harassment and finds there is a triable issue. The Court DENIES the 7th and 8th causes of action for failure to provide reasonable accommodation and failure to provide reasonable accommodation and engage in an interactive process. The Court finds they are triable issues as to whether the City fairly addressed the medical issues that were presented by Mr. Zhang. The Court GRANTS the 9th cause of action for retaliation, in favor of the City. The parties shall prepare form of order that complies with CCP 437C(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

ATTY. JENNIFER DONNELLAN, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-3917 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Jerrie Spencer's motion for reimbursement of costs for failure to appear at deposition is granted. Michael Kim, counsel for plaintiff Zhen Zhang must pay $1,401.80 to the City. The court adopts in full the recommendation and rationale in the judge pro tem's report. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. TERENCE J. HOWZELL, ESQ. of the Office of the City Attorney, Tel No. 415-554-3800 appearing for Defendant City and County of San Francisco;
No appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, the demurrer to the tenth cause of action for wrongful termination in violation of public policy is sustained without leave to amend. In Miklosy v. Regents of University of California, 44 Cal.4th 876, the California Supreme Court held that a state common law claim for wrongful termination in violation of public policy, known as a Tameny action, cannot be brought against a public entity. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
